content.verdict: Half Moon occupies a 400-acre estate seven miles east of Montego Bay — a sprawling luxury property organized around a perfect crescent of two-mile white-sand beach (the eponymous half moon), a Robert Trent Jones Sr. championship golf course, an equestrian centre with stables of show horses, and 197 rooms across the resort and the Eclipse all-suite wing. Half Moon has hosted Queen Elizabeth II, Princess Margaret, and generations of European royalty — the scale, the beach, and the Trent Jones golf set it apart from Jamaica's smaller boutique properties. The new Eclipse wing reimagines the resort for honeymoon couples seeking modern oceanfront luxury.
content.best_room: The Eclipse all-suite wing is the property's modern crown jewel — opened in 2021, all 57 suites are oceanfront with infinity-edge plunge pools on the private terraces and direct beach access. The Eclipse Beachfront Suites with private plunge pool are the right honeymoon choice. For couples wanting more traditional Half Moon character, the Royal Villas (private 2-7 bedroom homes with full staff) at the eastern end of the property are the splurge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival on the Half Moon Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Sangster (MBJ) — Half Moon is 25 minutes east, one of Jamaica's most convenient resort transfers. Welcome rum punch on the Eclipse suite terrace as the sun sets over the half-moon bay. First dinner at Delmare, the property's sea-front Mediterranean-Italian, then a walk down the two-mile beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach, Snorkel & Eclipse Pool
itinerary_7_nights.description: The two-mile half-moon beach is the property's defining feature — it never feels crowded because guests spread along its length. Morning snorkel on the offshore reef (gear included), lazy lunch at Seagrape on the beach, afternoon at the Eclipse infinity pool overlooking the sea. Couples massage at the Fern Tree Spa cottage.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Robert Trent Jones Sr. Golf
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half Moon's 18-hole Robert Trent Jones Sr. championship course is among the Caribbean's strongest — palm-lined fairways, ocean views from several holes, and well-maintained greens. Lessons available with the resort pro for honeymoon couples wanting to learn together. Non-golfing partners can spend the morning at the spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Equestrian Centre & Beach Ride
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half Moon's equestrian centre is unique among Jamaican resorts — full stables with trail rides through the property and the genuinely magical beach ride: horses ridden bareback into the Caribbean shallows. Even non-riders can do the gentle morning beach walk. Afternoon back at the Eclipse pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Rose Hall Great House & Bob Marley Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rose Hall Great House — the haunted plantation mansion of Annie Palmer the 'White Witch of Rose Hall' — is just 10 minutes east of Half Moon. The candlelit night tour is genuinely atmospheric. Combine with a half-day excursion 90 minutes inland to Nine Mile, Bob Marley's birthplace and mausoleum, for a meaningful Jamaica cultural day.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Negril Cliffs Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 75 minutes west to Negril for the day — Seven Mile Beach in the morning, then sunset at Rick's Café on the West End cliffs to watch the famous 35-foot cliff-divers. Late return to Half Moon along the coast road. Final dinner at Sugar Mill, the resort's fine-dining Caribbean restaurant in a 200-year-old converted sugar mill on the property.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Eclipse Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final breakfast on the Eclipse suite terrace overlooking the sea, last swim in the half-moon bay, and the easy 25-minute transfer to MBJ. Half Moon's scale means seven nights still leaves activities undone — the property genuinely benefits from longer stays.

caveats: Half Moon is family-friendly with a kids' club and family demographic — couples wanting a strictly adults-only honeymoon environment should consider Couples or Sandals or Jamaica Inn instead; Eclipse wing skews more adult but is not exclusively so.
The 400-acre property is large — getting between rooms, beach, dining, golf, and spa often requires a buggy or a 10-15 minute walk; this is not a compact intimate resort.
The Eclipse wing is genuinely modern and excellent, but the older sections of Half Moon vary in renovation status; specifically request Eclipse for the most consistent contemporary luxury experience.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Half Moon's Eclipse wing is genuinely strong for honeymooners — modern oceanfront suites with private plunge pools, direct beach access, and the resort-wide amenities (Trent Jones golf, equestrian, spa, two-mile beach) that smaller boutique hotels can't match. Couples wanting active honeymoon variety over intimate seclusion will find Half Moon ideal; couples wanting purely adults-only environments should look elsewhere.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Mid-December through April is dry season and peak — golf course conditions are best, sunny weather, calm seas. May and early June offer excellent value before summer humidity. August through October has hurricane risk. November is excellent shoulder season.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Eclipse Beachfront Suites with plunge pools book 3 to 5 months ahead for high season. The Royal Villas (private homes with staff) often book a year ahead, particularly for Christmas, New Year, and Easter. Off-season has good flexibility across all categories.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No — Half Moon is family-friendly with a kids' club and welcomes children. The Eclipse wing skews more adult and couples-focused but is not exclusively adult. Honeymooners who want a fully adults-only Jamaica experience should consider Couples Tower Isle, Sandals Royal Plantation, Jamaica Inn, or The Caves instead.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: An Eclipse Beachfront Suite with private plunge pool is the right honeymoon choice — modern, oceanfront, infinity-edge plunge pool on the terrace, and the most contemporary product on the property. The Royal Villas are the splurge for couples wanting their own private home with full staff. Avoid the older non-Eclipse sections for honeymoon stays.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Sangster International (MBJ) in Montego Bay is the gateway — Half Moon is just 25 minutes east, one of the most convenient resort transfers in Jamaica. Direct flights to MBJ run from most major North American and UK cities. The hotel arranges all transfers.
